 Flag for accurate computation.
 

Detailed Description

Homogeneous medium with Bloch‑periodic excitation/Green's function.

The constructor selects a periodic Green's function according to the number of provided lattice vectors. Currently a 2D periodic Green's function is created when two lattice vectors are given; otherwise a warning is printed (extension point for 1D/3D periodicity).

Definition at line 28 of file DomainHom3DPer.h.

Constructor & Destructor Documentation

◆ DomainHom3DPer()

DomainHom3DPer::DomainHom3DPer ( SurfaceMesh * inMesh,
int inDIndex,
dcmplx inEpsilon,
dcmplx inMu,
dcmplx inVacuumWavelength,
rvec blochVector,
std::vector< rvec > latticeVectors )

Construct a periodic homogeneous domain and select a periodic Green's function.

Parameters
inMeshSurface mesh containing the region inDIndex.
inDIndexRegion index within inMesh.
inEpsilonRelative permittivity of the homogeneous medium.
inMuRelative permeability of the homogeneous medium.
inVacuumWavelengthFree‑space wavelength.
blochVectorBloch vector used for Floquet–Bloch conditions.
latticeVectorsList of lattice vectors defining periodicity. If the list has size 2, a 2D periodic Green's function is created with these vectors.

If exactly two lattice vectors are provided, a 2D periodic homogeneous Green's function (GreenFHom3DPer2D) is instantiated. Otherwise, a warning is printed (hook for future 1D/3D periodic Green's function implementations).
